perturbation.original_text: Proprietary Information shall not include information that: (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of any disclosure or other action or inaction by Receiving Party in breach of this Agreement (including any disclosure or other action or inaction by Representatives of Receiving Party that would constitute a breach of this Agreement if undertaken by Receiving Party itself); (ii) is or becomes known or avai1able to Receiving Party or any of its Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a source (other than Disclosing Party or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates or Representatives) that, to the best of the knowledge of Receiving Party, is not prohibited from disclosing such Proprietary Information to Receiving Party by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation; or (iii) is or was independently developed by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives without violation of any obligation under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Proprietary Information shall not include information that: (i) is or becomes generally available to the public, regardless of the cause; (ii) is or becomes known or avai1able to Receiving Party or any of its Representatives on a non-confidential basis from any source; or (iii) is or was independently developed by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives, even if it violates an obligation under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states Proprietary Information shall not include information that becomes publicly available UNLESS it's due to a breach by the Receiving Party. The new version removes this condition, implying ALL publicly available information is excluded, even if the Receiving Party leaked it. This directly contradicts the original intent of protecting information even if the Receiving party is negligent. The original text also stated that the Proprietary Information shall not include information that was independently developed by Receiving Party without violation of any obligation under this Agreement, however with the changed text it mentions even if it violates an obligation under this agreement, the information will be considered as not Proprietary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party agrees that, due to the unique nature of the Proprietary Information, the unauthorized disclosure or use of the Proprietary Information may cause injury to Disclosing Party, the extent of which will be difficult to ascertain and for which there may be no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, Receiving Party agrees that Disclosing Company, in addition to any other available remedies, may have the right to seek an immediate injunction and other equitable relief enjoining any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement ordered by the court. Receiving Party shall notify Disclosing Party immediately if Receiving Party has reason to believe that any person who has had access to the Proprietary Information (including Receiving Party or any of its Representatives) has violated or intends to violate the terms of this Agreement or otherwise disclose any Proprietary Information in violation of the terms hereof. Any and all remedies herein expressly conferred upon a party will be deemed cumulative with and not exclusive of any other remedy conferred hereby, or by law or equity upon such party, and the exercise by a party of any one remedy will not preclude the exercise of any ether remedy.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in the Proprietary Information section significantly weakens the protection of the Disclosing Party's information. The original text stated that information becoming public due to the Receiving Party's breach would still be considered Proprietary Information. The changed text removes this exception, stating that ANY publicly available information is not considered Proprietary, even if the Receiving Party caused the disclosure. Also, the changed text mentions that information developed independently by the Receiving Party will not be considered Proprietary, even if it violates the terms of the agreement. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which allows the Disclosing Party to seek injunctions and other remedies for unauthorized disclosure or use of Proprietary Information. Because the change now states that even if the receiving party violates the terms of the agreement, they will not be held liable since it is no longer considered Proprietary information.

perturbation.original_text: Without the written consent of Disclosing Party and except as otherwise required by applicable law, Receiving Party shall keep, and shall cause its Representatives to keep, all Proprietary Information confidential and shall not disclose or reveal, and shall cause its Representatives not to disclose or reveal, in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, any Proprietary Information to any person, other than to its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in its evaluation of the Proposed Transaction or who otherwise need to know the Proprietary Information for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction and who are bound by restrictions regarding the disclosure and use of such Proprietary Information (either contractual, legal or fiduciary) owed to Company, Interested Party or any their respective Representatives that are comparable to and no less restrictive than those set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Without the written consent of Disclosing Party and except as otherwise required by applicable law, Receiving Party may, at its sole discretion, disclose Proprietary Information to any person, provided that the Receiving Party deems it necessary for the evaluation of the Proposed Transaction. Receiving Party shall not be held responsible for any disclosure or revelation of Proprietary Information by its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the Receiving Party 'may, at its sole discretion, disclose Proprietary Information' which directly contradicts the initial statement that the Receiving Party 'shall keep, and shall cause its Representatives to keep, all Proprietary Information confidential and shall not disclose or reveal'. Furthermore, it removes the responsibility of the Receiving Party for the actions of its Representatives, conflicting with the established obligation to ensure their Representatives also maintain confidentiality. Receiving Party can disclose anything, with the new changed_text, but on the previous definition of the same agreement, it's required to keep Proprietary Information confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure . Without the written consent of Disclosing Party and except as otherwise required by applicable law, Receiving Party shall keep, and shall cause its Representatives to keep, all Proprietary Information confidential and shall not disclose or reveal, and shall cause its Representatives not to disclose or reveal, in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, any Proprietary Information to any person, other than to its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in its evaluation of the Proposed Transaction or who otherwise need to know the Proprietary Information for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction and who are bound by restrictions regarding the disclosure and use of such Proprietary Information (either contractual, legal or fiduciary) owed to Company, Interested Party or any their respective Representatives that are comparable to and no less restrictive than those set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text, and the contradicted text, clearly state that the Receiving Party 'shall keep, and shall cause its Representatives to keep, all Proprietary Information confidential and shall not disclose or reveal'. The changed text says the Receiving Party 'may, at its sole discretion, disclose Proprietary Information'. This is a direct conflict, as the Receiving Party is now permitted to disclose information they were previously obligated to keep confidential. This is further compounded by the change absolving the Receiving Party of responsibility for its Representatives' actions, which contradicts the original obligation to ensure their confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall take the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential proprietary information of similar nature and importance (but in no event less than reasonable care) to protect the confidentiality and avoid the use, disclosure, publication or dissemination of the Proprietary Information of Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall take no specific degree of care to protect the confidentiality and avoid the use, disclosure, publication or dissemination of the Proprietary Information of Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires the Receiving Party to take at least reasonable care in protecting the Proprietary Information. The modification states the Receiving Party shall take *no specific degree of care*, thus contradicting the requirement to take reasonable care and essentially negating any obligation to safeguard the information.This is going against Receiving party's obligation of keeping confidential, since by not taking any care of that Proprietary Information, the receiving party has higher chances of being victim of an unauthorized disclosure. This is creating In-Text Contradiction with the entire section 2 of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure . Without the written consent of Disclosing Party and except as otherwise required by applicable law, Receiving Party shall keep, and shall cause its Representatives to keep, all Proprietary Information confidential and shall not disclose or reveal, and shall cause its Representatives not to disclose or reveal, in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, any Proprietary Information to any person, other than to its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in its evaluation of the Proposed Transaction or who otherwise need to know the Proprietary Information for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction and who are bound by restrictions regarding the disclosure and use of such Proprietary Information (either contractual, legal or fiduciary) owed to Company, Interested Party or any their respective Representatives that are comparable to and no less restrictive than those set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Receiving Party shall take *no specific degree of care* to protect Proprietary Information. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text which states that the Receiving Party *shall keep all Proprietary Information confidential and shall not disclose or reveal*. By negating any obligation to safeguard the information, the changed text makes it impossible for the Receiving Party to comply with the confidentiality requirement in the contradicted text. This creates a strong and direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Interested Party agrees that neither it nor its affiliates will at any time until the one year anniversary of the Effective Date, directly or indirectly, employ or solicit for employment (i) any key technical or management personnel of the Company that has first been introduced by the Company to the Interested Party in connection with the Proposed Transaction or who was otherwise substantively involved in the discussions of the Proposed Transaction or (ii) any other person who is now employed as an officer of the other party or any of its affiliates; provided, that the foregoing restrictions hall not be deemed to prohibit Interested Party or its Representatives from making general public solicitations for employment for any position or from employing any employee of the Company who either responds to such a general solicitation for employment or otherwise contacts Interested Party on his or her own initiative and without solicitation by Interested Party in contravention of the above restriction.
perturbation.changed_text: Interested Party may, at its discretion, employ or solicit for employment any personnel of the Company at any time, regardless of their involvement in the Proposed Transaction or their position within the Company. This includes key technical or management personnel, as well as officers of the Company or its affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause restricts the Interested Party from soliciting or employing certain Company personnel for one year after the Effective Date. The modified clause completely removes this restriction, allowing the Interested Party to employ or solicit any Company personnel at any time, directly contradicting the original agreement not to solicit or employ specific personnel. The modified version is going against No Solicitation of Employees definition, by making all employees to be directly solicited by the Interested Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Solicitation of Employees . Interested Party agrees that neither it nor its affiliates will at any time until the one year anniversary of the Effective Date, directly or indirectly, employ or solicit for employment (i) any key technical or management personnel of the Company that has first been introduced by the Company to the Interested Party in connection with the Proposed Transaction or who was otherwise substantively involved in the discussions of the Proposed Transaction or (ii) any other person who is now employed as an officer of the other party or any of its affiliates; provided, that the foregoing restrictions hall not be deemed to prohibit Interested Party or its Representatives from making general public solicitations for employment for any position or from employing any employee of the Company who either responds to such a general solicitation for employment or otherwise contacts Interested Party on his or her own initiative and without solicitation by Interested Party in contravention of the above restriction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows the Interested Party to employ or solicit any Company personnel at any time, directly contradicting the 'No Solicitation of Employees' clause in the contradicted text, which restricts such actions for a specific period (one year after the Effective Date) and for specific personnel. The change fundamentally alters the obligation, creating a clear in-text contradiction according to the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise provided herein, the obligations of this Agreement, including the restrictions on disclosure and use, shall terminate on the second anniversary of the Effective Date; provided that Sections 2.5, 2.6, and 2.7 and Articles 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 and 9 shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of this Agreement, including all restrictions on disclosure and use and all other articles and sections, shall terminate immediately upon the expression of disinterest in the Proposed Transaction by either party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause establishes a specific duration for the agreement (two years) and lists sections that survive termination. The modified clause introduces a new termination condition (expression of disinterest) and stipulates immediate termination of ALL obligations, including those previously designated to survive. This directly contradicts the specified survival clauses and the defined term length. This is creating uncertainty on the term of the obligations and use by having two different termination conditions, one is having two years, and the other is any time if someone expresses desinterest. Making this contradiction to impact article 8, where under the original agreement, after the term of the contract, Proprietary Information materials, needs to be returned or distroyed
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN OF MATERIALS. If either party hereto shall determine that it does not wish to proceed with the Proposed Transaction, such party shall promptly advise the other party of that decision. In that case, or if the Proposed Transaction otherwise is not consummated for any reason, Receiving Party shall, upon Disclosing Party’s written request, promptly deliver to Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information, and, at Disclosing Party’s sole election, return or destroy (provided that my such destruction shall be certified by a duly authorized Representative of Receiving Party) all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon (whether in hard-copy form or on intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files) in Receiving Party’s or any of its Representatives’ possession; provided , that if a legal proceeding has been instituted to seek disclosure of the Proprietary Information, such material shall not be destroyed until the proceeding is settled or a final judgment with respect thereto has been rendered. However, Receiving Party may retain in the office of its legal counsel, one copy of Proprietary Information for record purposes only. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of any Proprietary Information, or documents or material containing or reflecting any Proprietary Information, the parties will continue to be bound by their obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder for the term of this Agreement (or such other term as may be applicable to the specific obligation), except as otherwise specifically provided herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that sections 2.5, 2.6, and 2.7 and Articles 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 and 9 survive termination of the agreement. The changed text stipulates that all obligations terminate immediately upon expression of disinterest. This creates a direct conflict with the 'RETURN OF MATERIALS' clause in the Contradicted Text, which falls under Article 8 and which outlines obligations that continue even after termination under the original agreement. The changed text eliminates this continued obligation, creating a strong in-text contradiction as Article 8 obligations are no longer valid upon disinterest.
